title: Beacon Rule Marketplace (public preview)
description:
Customers want to share custom Beacon rules across orgs. Today, every team that wants a rule for, say, 'forbid `eval()` in user-input handlers' has to write it from scratch. The Marketplace lets one team publish a rule and any other customer subscribe to it — versioned, attributable, with usage signals.

Scope is **public preview**: read-only browsing for everyone, publishing gated to Business+ tier, no monetization (free for now), no curation team.

Full GA is a separate epic; this is the smallest thing that gets the network effect started.
acceptance_criteria: Customers can publish a rule from their org to the public marketplace
Customers can browse and subscribe to published rules
Subscribed rules update via semver: minor/patch auto-applies, major requires manual confirmation
Usage telemetry: how many orgs subscribed to each rule, rule fire rate aggregate (privacy-preserved)
All Marketplace surfaces accessible from app.pylon.dev/marketplace
Doc page at pylon.dev/docs/marketplace
Launch blog post drafted by Marketing

comments.author: emp_003
comments.body: Filing this per the Q1 commit. Wei to lead; Tomás's team owns the publishing UX. Renata to drive the customer comms plan since 8 of our top accounts have asked for this.

comments.author: emp_011
comments.body: On it. Going to keep this narrow. No marketplace for *rule packs* in the preview — single rules only. Pack-level versioning is a 6-month problem we don't need to solve yet.

comments.author: emp_002
comments.body: +1 on narrow. The interesting risk here is rule quality — if a customer publishes a rule with a 50% false-positive rate and 20 other customers subscribe, we get a brand problem. Suggesting we surface fire-rate + dismissal-rate in the marketplace listing from day one.

comments.author: emp_011
comments.body: Yes — moving that into AC. Privacy-preserved aggregate (no per-customer rates exposed).

comments.author: emp_001
comments.body: Public preview shipped today. 23 rules published in the first 4 days. Calling this epic done. GA epic to be filed separately once we see how the preview behaves through Q2.

title: GitLab Cloud integration (Switchyard, GA)
description:
Pylon sells primarily to GitHub teams today. ~30% of qualified leads we lose cite 'we use GitLab.' Adding GitLab Cloud as a fully-supported platform is the second-biggest unlock in the pipeline (after Java).

GA bar:
- Webhook ingest works on the same SLA as GitHub
- Comment posting, merge-block enforcer, and rule analysis run with feature parity
- OAuth + token refresh round-trip handles GitLab's specific quirks
- Documentation is complete (install guide, troubleshooting)
- One Enterprise prospect has run a 2-week pilot end-to-end without an outage

Non-goals for this epic: GitLab self-hosted (Enterprise), GitLab Premium-only features.
acceptance_criteria: Customer can install Pylon via GitLab Cloud OAuth in under 5 minutes
Pylon comments on MRs (merge requests) with the same UX as GitHub PRs
Pylon enforces merge blocks via GitLab status checks
Webhook latency p95 < 1.5s (matches GitHub)
Analyzer pipeline runs Beacon on MR diffs same as GitHub PRs
Documentation published at pylon.dev/docs/gitlab
At least one Enterprise pilot completes 2-week trial without P0/P1 incident

comments.author: emp_010
comments.body: Picking this up. Going to break it across S2-S14. Webhook ingest first, then comment posting (the trickiest piece because GitLab's MR comment API is different enough to surprise you), then OAuth, then merge-block.

comments.author: emp_004
comments.body: Sales pipeline angle: 4 deals currently in flight where GitLab support is the gate. Will keep CRM updated as this progresses.

comments.author: emp_010
comments.body: Webhook + comment posting + OAuth all done as of S7. MR analyzer pipeline scheduled for S11. Then merge-block in S13. Pilot prospect (Verdant Energy) signed for late S13 trial.

comments.author: emp_010
comments.body: Verdant Energy pilot in flight; 1 P2 found (PYL-305 — duplicate comments on retry, fix in flight). No P0/P1. On track for end-of-S14 GA.

comments.author: emp_032
comments.body: From CS — Verdant signed off on the pilot today. Their VP Eng said the comment posting feels indistinguishable from GitHub. We're good for GA contingent on the PYL-305 fix shipping in S14.

title: Tower team comparison views
description:
Engineering managers want to compare review-quality metrics across teams within their org without exporting data to a spreadsheet. Today, Tower shows per-team data but you have to switch context to see another team. Cross-team comparison is the most-requested Tower feature in customer interviews (n=14, Q4 2025).

Scope: side-by-side comparison view of up to 4 teams on a single dashboard. Metrics: cycle time, review depth distribution, hotspot density. Out of scope: cross-org comparison (privacy concerns), benchmark vs. industry (separate PRDB epic, far future).
acceptance_criteria: User can select 2-4 teams from their org and see a side-by-side dashboard
Three metrics rendered: cycle time, review depth distribution, hotspot density
Permissions check: user must have view access to all selected teams
Dashboard exportable as PNG / PDF for QBR-style use
Performance: dashboard loads in < 2s for orgs with up to 50 teams

comments.author: emp_022
comments.body: Picking this up. Will start with the data API in S9, dashboard UI in S12-13, permissions in S14.

comments.author: emp_021
comments.body: Design draft posted in Figma (link omitted — internal). Felix is the lead designer. Two open questions: (1) do we show absolute numbers or only relative deltas? (2) what's the empty state when only one team is selected? Will discuss in product review Friday.

comments.author: emp_003
comments.body: From product review: absolute numbers + a relative-delta toggle. Empty state shows 'select another team to compare' CTA. Approved.

comments.author: emp_022
comments.body: Permissions piece is the long pole. SAML group resolution is gnarlier than I expected. Targeting EOS14 still feasible but tight.

title: Audit logs (Business tier)
description:
Audit logs are a Business-tier feature that customers need for SOC 2 reviews of their own (Pylon as their tool). Currently we don't expose any audit trail; customers asking for SOC 2 evidence have to email support and we manually pull from server logs. Embarrassing.

Scope: log every rule change, every dashboard-config change, every billing change, every user permission change. Retention: 1 year on Business, 7 years on Enterprise. Export: CSV and JSON. UI: dedicated audit log view in the Settings panel, filterable by event type, user, date range.
acceptance_criteria: All in-app mutations logged: rule create/update/delete, config change, permission change, billing change
Log entries include: event type, actor (user_id), timestamp, before/after diff (where applicable), source IP
UI: filterable view in Settings → Audit Logs
Export: CSV + JSON, paginated for large exports
Retention: 1y Business / 7y Enterprise
SOC 2 Type II evidence integration: auditor can pull current evidence via Enterprise portal (separate ticket)

comments.author: emp_012
comments.body: Taking this. Will own end-to-end. Renata flagged this as the #1 ask from CS this quarter.

comments.author: emp_002
comments.body: One thing to be careful with: don't log raw rule content into the audit log if it might contain PII (some custom rules contain customer-specific identifiers in their match patterns). Diff should be 'rule X updated' not 'rule X content was: <content>'.

comments.author: emp_012
comments.body: Good catch. Adding to AC. Will sanitize.

comments.author: emp_001
comments.body: Shipped. Closing. Renata, please push this to the customers who'd been asking.

title: Python async-correctness rule pack
description:
Customer pain log shows async-correctness bugs as the most-frequently-cited 'bug class our review missed' across Python customers. Examples: forgotten `await`, sync I/O in async context, blocking event loop with CPU-bound code, using `requests` in async functions.

Scope: 30 new Python rules across asyncio, httpx, aiohttp, and general async patterns. Roughly 10 rules per sprint over 3 sprints. Each rule needs: detection logic, a test corpus of ~20 known true-positives + 20 known false-positives, customer-facing rule docs page, severity calibration.
acceptance_criteria: 30 new Python rules merged into Beacon's default rule pack
Each rule has: detection, test corpus (≥40 examples), docs page
Aggregate false-positive rate ≤8% across the 30 rules in shadow mode
Customers opted into shadow mode see findings before rules go live
Released in Beacon 2.7

comments.author: emp_080
comments.body: Owning this. Going to start with the highest-frequency patterns from the customer pain log. The 10 most-cited will land in S13. Async-with-httpx is the biggest one — most customers have at least one of these in their codebases.

comments.author: emp_011
comments.body: +1. Recommend we shadow-mode the first 10 for 2 weeks before promoting to default-on. The async stuff has weird false-positive surface area around `asyncio.gather` patterns.

comments.author: emp_080
comments.body: First 10 shipped in S13 — 6.4% FP rate in shadow mode. Promoting to default-on this sprint. Next 20 in flight.

title: Pylon Suggest — research, scoping, gated beta plan
description:
Pylon Suggest is the working name for AI-generated fix suggestions attached to Beacon comments. Customer ask is consistent (we hear it every quarter). The bar for shipping is high: a wrong suggestion is a bigger brand cost than no suggestion at all. The Master PRD lists this as 'late 2026' specifically because we want the research and scoping work to be done well before any code ships.

This epic is the research + scoping phase: figure out which rule subsets are mechanically suggestable (vs. those that aren't), how to surface the suggestion in PR comments without overwhelming reviewers, and what the gate is between gated beta (Q4 2026) and broader access (Q1 2027).
acceptance_criteria: Document published: 'Which Beacon rules are suggest-ready' (taxonomy + criteria)
Design spec for the suggestion UI in PR comments (Felix + Wei collaboration)
Gated beta entry criteria documented (which customers, which rules, opt-in flow)
Risk register: known failure modes + mitigation

comments.author: emp_011
comments.body: I'll lead. Spike PYL-500 will land in S9 (which rules are suggest-ready). Then we'll have follow-up spikes / planning into S13-14.

comments.author: emp_002
comments.body: Tagging this with our commitment from the board update: no GA in 2026, gated beta only. Want to make sure we don't get pulled toward shipping faster than the rule semantics support.

comments.author: emp_011
comments.body: Spike done (PYL-500); rule taxonomy in flight. UX spec coming next sprint. Risk register draft circulated; will be reviewed by Olivia, Marcus, Priya before gated-beta entry criteria are locked.

title: Marketplace data model + Postgres schema
description: Foundation work for the Marketplace: design the Postgres tables (rules, rule_versions, subscriptions, publications), write migrations, set up the rule-version semver enforcement at the schema level.
acceptance_criteria: Migrations applied for: marketplace_rules, marketplace_rule_versions, marketplace_subscriptions
Foreign keys + cascading delete behavior reviewed
Semver enforcement: version field validated as semver, `latest` view computed from MAX semver per rule
Indexes on (rule_id, version), (subscriber_org_id), (publisher_org_id)
Schema diff applied to staging and verified

comments.author: emp_080
comments.body: Posted draft schema in #beacon channel. Two open questions: (1) should rule content be stored as JSONB or text? (2) do we need soft-deletes or hard?

comments.author: emp_011
comments.body: JSONB. Easier to query later. Soft-delete: yes — customers may want to unsubscribe-then-resubscribe; we want to preserve their history.

comments.author: emp_081
comments.body: Quick infra note — make sure the migration runs in <30s on prod or we need a backfill plan. The marketplace_rules table will be sparse initially but the indexes can be slow to build at scale.

comments.author: emp_080
comments.body: Merged. ~12s migration on staging with 0 rules; should be fine in prod.

title: Rule publishing UX
description: Build the dashboard surface that lets a customer publish one of their custom rules to the public marketplace. Form-based: rule selector, public name, description, license selector (default: Apache 2.0), version field (semver-validated). On submit: validation, preview, confirm.
acceptance_criteria: Form rendered at app.pylon.dev/marketplace/publish
Required fields: rule selector (dropdown of org's custom rules), public name, description (≥50 chars), license, version
Preview screen showing what subscribers will see
Validation: semver, name uniqueness, description length, license enum
On submit: rule + first version inserted, publisher org tagged
Empty state: 'You don't have custom rules to publish yet' CTA to rule authoring

comments.author: emp_090
comments.body: Owning. Kai's design is clean — single page, no wizard. Ship it as a one-shot.

comments.author: emp_021
comments.body: Note on the description field: enforce ≥50 chars at submission, but show the count live as user types so they don't get a surprise rejection.

comments.author: emp_090
comments.body: Merged. PYL-304 caught a Safari regression I'll handle separately.

title: Rule subscription flow
description: Subscription flow: customer browses marketplace, clicks 'subscribe' on a rule, picks how it integrates (default-on, default-off, or shadow mode for 2 weeks). Persist subscription, install rule into their config on next config compile.
acceptance_criteria: Subscribe button on marketplace rule detail page
Modal: integration mode (on/off/shadow), shadow-period selector if shadow
Subscription persisted with mode + period
Rule appears in customer's `pylon.yaml` (auto-PR posted to their repo)
Unsubscribe is reachable from settings
Audit log entry created (PYL-103 dependency)

comments.author: emp_012
comments.body: Will own. Going to coordinate with PYL-201 author so they share components.

comments.author: emp_032
comments.body: From CS perspective: please make sure the subscription confirmation email is templated so we can include a link to the rule's docs page. Subscribers will frequently ask 'wait, what does this rule do?' a week after they subscribed.

comments.author: emp_012
comments.body: Merged. Email template links to rule docs ✅

title: Rule versioning + semver auto-update behavior
description: Implement the semver behavior for marketplace rules: minor/patch updates auto-apply to subscribers; major updates require explicit confirmation. Surface 'update available' notifications in the dashboard. New rule version metadata stored in `marketplace_rule_versions`.
acceptance_criteria: On new rule version published: subscribers' `pylon.yaml` auto-updates if minor/patch
Major version: notification in dashboard, no auto-apply
Customer can pin to specific version if they don't want auto-updates
Rule version changelog rendered on rule detail page

comments.author: emp_080
comments.body: Edge case to think about: what if a rule has a deprecated_in_version field? We should warn subscribers before the rule actually becomes a no-op.

comments.author: emp_011
comments.body: Add to follow-up; not blocking for v0.1 of the marketplace. We'll have very few deprecations in the first 90 days.

title: Marketplace browse UI
description: Browse-and-search UI for the marketplace. Filters: language, category (security, async, perf, style), publisher org. Sort by: subscribers, fire rate (lower = stronger signal), most recent. Detail page per rule with description, examples, dismissal-rate aggregate, subscribe CTA.
acceptance_criteria: /marketplace landing page with grid of rules
Filter sidebar: language, category, publisher, license
Search bar (full-text on name + description)
Sort options: most subscribers, fire rate, newest
Detail page per rule: description, examples, fire rate aggregate, subscribe button
Empty state: 'No rules match these filters'

comments.author: emp_012
comments.body: Felix and I aligned on layout. Going dense by default (5-column grid on desktop). Customers told us the marketplace will only feel useful with critical mass; keeping the UI dense to make it feel populated.

comments.author: emp_021
comments.body: +1 on dense. We can revisit if it feels overwhelming once we have 200+ rules.

title: Public preview launch coordination + comms
description: Coordinate the public preview launch comms: blog post, email to existing customers, social media posts, dev community outreach (HN, Lobsters, r/programming).
acceptance_criteria: Blog post drafted (Lila), reviewed (Sasha + Mei), published on launch day
Email to existing Business+ tier customers about new ability to publish
Social posts scheduled: launch day + day 3 (showing first 5 published rules)
HN submission timed for 9am PT launch day

comments.author: emp_051
comments.body: Will draft. Pulling examples from the 5 rules we've already had partner customers publish (in private beta) so the launch post has real artifacts to point at, not abstractions.

comments.author: emp_005
comments.body: Launch went well. HN front page for ~6 hours, LinkedIn engagement strong. 23 published rules in first 4 days — better than the 10-rule target we'd set internally.

title: GitLab webhook ingest
description: Implement GitLab webhook ingest in Switchyard. Subscribe to MR events (opened, updated, merged), comment events, and approval events. Verify signatures using GitLab's HMAC-SHA256 scheme. Route to the analyzer pipeline same as GitHub.
acceptance_criteria: Switchyard endpoint accepts GitLab webhook payloads
HMAC-SHA256 signature verification
Event types ingested: merge_request, note (comment), approval
Payload normalized to internal schema (same as GitHub events)
Latency p95 < 1.5s
Failure handling: retry with exponential backoff, dead-letter after 5 failures

comments.author: emp_010
comments.body: GitLab's webhook is similar to GitHub's but with different event names and payload shapes. Going to write an adapter layer that normalizes both into our internal event schema. That way the downstream pipeline doesn't care which platform the event came from.

comments.author: emp_081
comments.body: Nit: GitLab's webhook signature header is `X-Gitlab-Token`, not `X-Hub-Signature` like GitHub. Easy to miss.

comments.author: emp_010
comments.body: Merged. Adapter pattern works cleanly; downstream pipeline didn't change.

title: GitLab MR comment poster
description: Post Pylon's findings as MR comments via GitLab's API. Match the GitHub UX as closely as possible: in-line on the diff for line-specific findings, summary comment at the top of the MR for non-line findings.
acceptance_criteria: Pylon comments posted to GitLab MRs via gitlab.com/api/v4 (or self-hosted equivalent for Cloud)
In-line comments anchored to specific lines
Summary comment at MR top with finding count and links
Comment styling matches Pylon's GitHub UX
Idempotent: re-posting the same finding doesn't create duplicates

comments.author: emp_010
comments.body: GitLab's comment API has a quirk where 'discussion' is the parent of 'note' — different mental model from GitHub's 'review' / 'comment'. Need to map cleanly.

comments.author: emp_080
comments.body: FYI we already had a customer (Lumen Health) ask whether GitLab support means they can suppress findings via comment annotations like they do on GitHub. Yes. Should be transparent to the rule engine.

title: GitLab OAuth + token refresh
description: OAuth integration with GitLab Cloud. Handle token refresh (access tokens expire after 2 hours), revocation, and re-auth on permission changes.
acceptance_criteria: Customer can install Pylon via GitLab Cloud OAuth
Access token + refresh token persisted (encrypted at rest)
Auto-refresh before expiry
Revocation flow: customer can disconnect from Pylon dashboard or from GitLab side
Permission scope changes detected and customer prompted to re-auth

comments.author: emp_081
comments.body: Picking up. GitLab Cloud's OAuth is straightforward but the 2h token lifetime means we need a robust refresh background job. Will add to the existing auth-refresh worker.

comments.author: emp_010
comments.body: Let's make sure the refresh failure case is loud — if we silently lose access to a customer's GitLab, all their MR comments stop posting and they won't notice for hours.

comments.author: emp_081
comments.body: Added Sentry alert on persistent refresh failure (3+ in a row) + customer-facing 'Pylon needs to be reconnected' banner. Merged.

title: GitLab MR analyzer pipeline integration
description: Wire GitLab MR diffs into Beacon's analyzer pipeline. Beacon already runs against PR diffs from GitHub via the normalized event schema; this story closes the loop by ensuring GitLab MRs trigger analyzer runs and findings flow back to the comment poster (PYL-207).
acceptance_criteria: GitLab MR open/update events trigger Beacon analyzer run
Findings posted via PYL-207 comment poster
Analyzer latency comparable to GitHub PRs (p95 < 90s)
Findings de-duplicated across MR updates

comments.author: emp_011
comments.body: From Beacon side: no changes needed if event normalization is clean. Looking forward to the QA pass.

comments.author: emp_010
comments.body: Merged. P95 latency 78s on staging, comfortable margin.

title: GitLab merge-block enforcer
description: Pylon-as-required-status-check on GitLab MRs. Match GitHub behavior: customer can configure Pylon as a merge gate; MR can't merge until Pylon's analyzer run completes.
acceptance_criteria: Pylon registered as a status check on GitLab MRs
Customer-configurable: required vs. informational
Status updates to 'success' or 'failure' based on analyzer findings + customer's threshold config
Test coverage on the enforcer branch logic

comments.author: emp_010
comments.body: PR up. Reviewing in pair with Hugo. One open question: GitLab has an 'unblock' mechanism where a project owner can override a failed status check. Do we want to log this as a Pylon override event in the audit log? Tagging Tomás.

comments.author: emp_012
comments.body: Yes. Audit log should capture overrides. Would be a SOC 2 finding if we don't.

title: GitLab GA documentation
description: Write the customer-facing docs for GitLab Cloud. Sections: install guide, troubleshooting, differences-from-GitHub callout, FAQ.
acceptance_criteria: Install guide step-by-step (with screenshots)
Troubleshooting page: token issues, webhook failures, comment-not-appearing
FAQ: 'Why does my MR show two Pylon comments?', 'Why is the analyzer slow on first run?', etc.
Published at pylon.dev/docs/gitlab on GA day

comments.author: emp_052
comments.body: Going to mirror the GitHub doc structure. Where the platforms differ, callouts. Easier for customers who use both.

comments.author: emp_010
comments.body: FYI — adjust the troubleshooting section for the duplicate-comment issue. PYL-305 fix should be in by GA, but pre-fix customers may still hit it briefly.

title: GitLab go-live customer pilot (Verdant Energy)
description: 2-week pilot with Verdant Energy (Enterprise prospect). End-to-end Pylon on their GitLab Cloud-backed engineering org. They have 65 engineers; ~120 MRs/week; primarily Python and Go.
acceptance_criteria: Pilot installed week-1 of S14
All MRs in pilot period analyzed by Beacon
Daily check-in with customer engineering lead
Issues triaged and resolved within 24h or escalated
Pilot report delivered to customer at end of S14
Verdant signs Enterprise contract or provides specific go/no-go feedback

comments.author: emp_032
comments.body: Owning pilot CS-side. Daily check-ins with their VP Eng, weekly with their CTO. First week analysis: 437 MRs analyzed, 12 findings dismissed as FP, 4 confirmed bugs caught (one was a critical async-correctness issue in their billing service).

comments.author: emp_032
comments.body: Verdant signed off on the pilot. Their VP Eng said the comment posting feels indistinguishable from GitHub. We're good for GA contingent on PYL-305 fix shipping in S14. Contract negotiations in progress with sales.

title: Tower team comparison data API
description: Build the API endpoint that returns multi-team comparison data: cycle time, review depth, hotspot density across N teams. Queryable by org_id + list of team_ids + date range.
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/v1/tower/team-comparison endpoint
Query params: org_id, team_ids (1-4), since, until
Response includes the 3 metrics per team
Auth: requires org-level access; team-level filtering applied based on user's permissions
Performance: < 500ms p95 for orgs with 50 teams and 90-day window

comments.author: emp_022
comments.body: Going to start with a denormalized read model in ClickHouse. Querying live across multiple teams' data is too slow.

comments.author: emp_011
comments.body: +1 on denormalized. Tower reads should never be slow.

title: Tower team comparison dashboard UI
description: Side-by-side dashboard for comparing 2-4 teams. Uses the API from PYL-213. Three charts (cycle time, review depth, hotspot density) rendered side-by-side, with team-color encoding. Toggle for absolute vs. relative-delta view.
acceptance_criteria: /dashboard/teams/compare route
Team picker (max 4)
Three charts side-by-side, color-coded per team
Absolute / relative toggle
Export as PNG, PDF
Loads in < 2s for 4 teams + 90-day data

comments.author: emp_090
comments.body: Felix designed this. Going with the relative-delta toggle prominent in the top-right. Felt natural in the Figma walkthrough.

comments.author: emp_012
comments.body: Merged. Permissions story (PYL-215) is the only remaining piece for full launch.

title: Permissions for cross-team viewing
description: User must have view access to all teams they're comparing. Resolve via SAML group membership for SAML-enabled orgs; via Pylon-native team membership for others. Edge case: if user loses access to one team mid-session, the comparison should drop that team, not error out.
acceptance_criteria: Permission check on each team_id submitted to the comparison endpoint
SAML group resolution for SAML-enabled orgs
Native team-membership for others
Graceful degradation: if user loses access to team mid-session, drop from view with notice
Audit log entry: 'team comparison viewed' with team_ids

comments.author: emp_022
comments.body: SAML group resolution is the gnarly part. Customers configure their groups in many ways; we need to be flexible without being overspecified.

comments.author: emp_080
comments.body: FYI: I hit a similar SAML quirk on PYL-104 work — make sure you're using the resolved group memberships from the JWT claim, not the raw IdP attribute. The latter has caching issues we already patched once.

title: Demo + sales-enablement materials for team comparison
description: Create demo materials for the GA launch of team comparison. Recorded walkthrough, sales deck slides, customer-facing one-pager.
acceptance_criteria: 5-minute recorded walkthrough by Jake
3 sales-deck slides covering the new feature
One-page customer brief (PDF) with use cases
Talking points for AEs

title: Audit log event schema + ingest
description: Define the audit log event schema (Postgres table) and write the ingest path. Every in-app mutation should emit an event with: type, actor, timestamp, before/after diff, IP. Use append-only table; no updates or deletes (compliance requirement).
acceptance_criteria: audit_log table created (append-only)
Ingest function callable from any service: emit_audit_event()
Events captured: rule_create, rule_update, rule_delete, config_change, permission_change, billing_change
PII sanitization for rule content (per Priya's earlier note)
Ingest rate: handle ≥1000 events/sec without backpressure

comments.author: emp_081
comments.body: Append-only via revoking UPDATE/DELETE on the table from the application role; only DBA role can mutate. Postgres-level enforcement, not just convention.

comments.author: emp_002
comments.body: +1. Audit logs that can be edited by the app are useless for compliance.

comments.author: emp_012
comments.body: Schema landed. Will hook into the various services across S6-S8.

title: Audit log UI (Business tier)
description: Render the audit log in a settings panel. Filterable by event type, user, date range. Paginated. Available only on Business+ tier (gated).
acceptance_criteria: /settings/audit-log route, gated to Business+ tier
Table view: event type, actor, timestamp, summary
Filters: event type (multi), actor (multi), date range
Pagination (50/page) with cursor-based loading
Click row to expand full diff

comments.author: emp_021
comments.body: Let's not bury the diff under a click — show it inline if it's <5 lines. Most customers will scan visually rather than click each row.

title: Audit log export to CSV / JSON
description: Export audit log to CSV or JSON. Background job for large exports (>10k rows). Email when ready.
acceptance_criteria: Export button on audit log page
Format selector: CSV or JSON
Background job for >10k rows
Email notification when export ready
Export download link valid for 24h, signed URL

comments.author: emp_012
comments.body: PYL-303 came in mid-implementation: the query timed out on >100k rows. Backporting the streaming-export approach now.

title: SOC 2 Type II evidence integration prep
description: Prepare the audit log for Q3 2026 SOC 2 Type II evidence integration. Auditor portal access (Enterprise-only) will pull current evidence directly. This story is the prep work — schema review, retention policy enforcement, evidence query API.
acceptance_criteria: Retention policy enforced: 1y Business, 7y Enterprise
Evidence query API: returns audit log entries by control area
Documented mapping: audit log event types → SOC 2 controls
Auditor portal stub (full UI in Q3)

comments.author: emp_081
comments.body: Going to align with our auditor (PenetrationTest Inc) on what they want from the API. Saves rework later.

comments.author: emp_060
comments.body: Auditor confirmed the API shape. We're good. Q3 audit window opens July 1.

title: Async rule pack research + spec
description: Survey the customer pain log for async-correctness bug patterns. Write the spec for the 30-rule pack. Calibrate severity levels based on bug-cost-when-shipped data from customer postmortems.
acceptance_criteria: Customer pain log surveyed; top 30 patterns documented
Each rule has: pattern description, severity recommendation, expected FP rate, example
Spec reviewed by Wei + Hugo + Priya

comments.author: emp_080
comments.body: Surveyed 200 customer postmortems from the past 18 months. ~32% involve async-correctness. Documenting.

comments.author: emp_002
comments.body: 32% is high enough that this is unambiguously the right thing to invest in. Approving the spec.

title: First 10 async rules (asyncio + httpx + aiohttp basics)
description: Implement the first 10 rules from the spec. Focus on highest-frequency patterns: forgotten `await`, sync I/O in async, `requests` in async, blocking sleep, `asyncio.gather` exception handling, `asyncio.create_task` reference loss, etc. Each rule with test corpus.
acceptance_criteria: 10 rules merged into Beacon default rule pack
Each rule has ≥40 test examples (20 TP + 20 FP)
Aggregate FP rate ≤8% in shadow mode against last 30 days of customer PR data
Docs page per rule

comments.author: emp_080
comments.body: Shipped. FP rate in shadow mode: 6.4%. Promoting to default-on this sprint.

title: Next 20 async rules (gather/wait/timeout patterns)
description: Implement the remaining 20 rules from PYL-221 spec. Includes gather-with-return-exceptions patterns, wait-with-timeout patterns, asyncio.Lock/Semaphore misuse, and async-context-manager edge cases.
acceptance_criteria: 20 rules merged into Beacon default rule pack
Each rule has ≥40 test examples
Aggregate FP rate ≤8% in shadow mode
Docs pages

comments.author: emp_080
comments.body: 13 rules merged. Last 7 in flight. The asyncio.Lock patterns are tricky — they need control-flow analysis the simpler tree-sitter queries can't handle. Moved those into a follow-up.

title: Per-rule fire rate visibility in Beacon dashboard
description: Customer ask (Stratosphere Robotics, others): show per-rule fire rate on the Beacon rules listing page so customers can identify candidates for tuning or disabling.
acceptance_criteria: Rules listing page shows fire rate per rule (last 30 days)
Sortable by fire rate
'Tune this rule' link if FR > 15%

comments.author: emp_032
comments.body: Filing on behalf of Stratosphere — they brought it up in the QBR last week. Easy win.

title: Pylon CI status check name customization
description: Customer ask (Kaltsworth Logistics + 3 others): allow customers to customize the name of Pylon's status check on PRs. Their internal CI gates use specific status-check names; default 'Pylon' clashes with one customer's preexisting check.
acceptance_criteria: Settings UI: customize Pylon status check name (default 'Pylon')
Validation: alphanumeric + dashes, max 30 chars
Updates apply to all repos in the org

comments.author: emp_010
comments.body: Trivial change. Done.

title: GitHub App reinstall loses webhook subscription
description:
Customer (Lumen Health) reported: after reinstalling the Pylon GitHub App on their repos, no PR comments were posting. Investigation: webhook subscription wasn't being re-registered on App reinstall — only on initial install.

Production impact: 11 customers affected over the past 6 weeks (we can identify them via missing webhook events). Fix needed urgent.

comments.author: emp_033
comments.body: Lumen's VP Eng sent a Slack message at 5am ET. They've been seeing no Pylon comments for ~3 weeks since their security team forced a fresh App install. Engineering on it.

comments.author: emp_010
comments.body: Root cause: our install handler only registers webhook subscriptions on the `installation.created` event. Reinstall fires `installation_repositories.added` instead — different event, we weren't handling it.

comments.author: emp_081
comments.body: Backfill plan: identify all customers with `installation_repositories.added` events in our log over the past 90 days where webhook subscription is missing. Re-register. ~11 customers identified.

comments.author: emp_010
comments.body: Fix merged. Backfill running now. Lumen confirmed comments are flowing again.

comments.author: emp_033
comments.body: All 11 affected customers backfilled. Lumen sent a thank-you. Closing.

title: Tower cycle-time chart shows 0 for repos with single dev
description: Customer reports cycle-time chart shows 0h for repos where the same developer authored and merged the PR (no review by another person). Should show actual time, not skip.

comments.author: emp_022
comments.body: Yep, our cycle-time calculation excludes 'self-reviews' which is the wrong call. Fixing.

comments.author: emp_022
comments.body: Fix shipped. Recomputing cycle time across last 90 days for affected customers.

title: Beacon python.bare-except triggers on test fixtures with intentional bare-except
description: Customer (Arcola Studios) has Python test fixtures that intentionally use bare-except to simulate broad exception swallowing. Pylon flags every one of these. They've been suppressing line-by-line; tedious. Should respect the customer's path-based exclusions or have a smarter default for `tests/` paths.

comments.author: emp_080
comments.body: Adjusted rule scope to skip `tests/`, `**/test_*.py`, `**/*_test.py`, `**/conftest.py` by default. Customers can override via config if they want it on test files.

title: Audit log query times out on >100k rows
description: One Enterprise customer (Stratosphere Robotics) hit a 30-second timeout when filtering audit log to the past 12 months. They have ~150k entries.

comments.author: emp_012
comments.body: Index missing on (org_id, event_type, created_at). Adding. Also moving the export path off the synchronous query.

comments.author: emp_081
comments.body: +1 on the index. We should also limit the in-page query to 30-day windows and force the user to use export for longer windows.

comments.author: emp_012
comments.body: Index added. Page-query window capped at 30 days. Export now runs as background job. Closed.

title: Marketplace publishing form drops description on Safari
description: On Safari, the description textarea in the marketplace publishing form clears its contents on the first keystroke after pasting. Confirmed on Safari 17.x. Chrome and Firefox unaffected.

comments.author: emp_090
comments.body: TanStack Form interaction with Safari's input event quirks. Worked around with a controlled-input rewrite. Fix shipped.

title: GitLab MR comment posts twice on retry
description: Pilot customer (Verdant Energy) reported duplicate Pylon comments on some MRs. Investigation: when our analyzer pipeline retries on a transient failure, the comment poster doesn't dedupe against existing posted comments on GitLab. GitHub doesn't have this bug because we use the GitHub Reviews API which has built-in idempotency.

comments.author: emp_010
comments.body: Need an idempotency layer on the GitLab side — fetch existing notes, dedupe by stable hash before posting. Will land before GA.

comments.author: emp_032
comments.body: Verdant's confirmed they've seen ~6 instances of this in the pilot. They're tolerant since it's a pilot, but we definitely don't want this in GA.

comments.author: emp_010
comments.body: Fix in PR review. Targeting merge by 2026-04-25.

title: Switchyard webhook signature verification fails for new GitHub App key rotation
description:
GitHub rotated the signing key for our App last night (per their announced 90-day rotation policy). Webhook signature verification started failing for ~40% of webhooks at 2026-04-22 03:00 UTC. We don't have automatic key-fetch from GitHub's JWKS endpoint; we'd hardcoded the key. Production incident.

Mitigation: rotated to the new key manually at 04:30 UTC. Backlog of failed webhooks replayed by 05:15 UTC. No findings lost.

Fix: implement automatic JWKS rotation on a 12h refresh cycle.

comments.author: emp_010
comments.body: P1 incident. Recovered with manual rotation. Postmortem scheduled for Thursday. Need automatic rotation or this happens again in 90 days.

comments.author: emp_081
comments.body: Owning the auto-rotate work. Will use GitHub's JWKS endpoint at api.github.com/meta/public-keys (it's actually on a separate path, looking up exact).

comments.author: emp_002
comments.body: Postmortem owner: Ravi. Want a doc by Friday. The 'we hardcoded a rotating key' framing should appear prominently — we knew this would rotate and we let it slide.

comments.author: emp_081
comments.body: Auto-rotate implementation in PR review. Targeting merge tomorrow.

title: Upgrade FastAPI 0.110 → 0.115
description: Upgrade FastAPI from 0.110 to 0.115. Includes a few small breaking changes around Pydantic v2 compatibility but most code is unaffected.

comments.author: emp_081
comments.body: Done. One Pydantic schema needed `model_validator` rename. Tests passing.

title: Migrate pgvector 0.5 → 0.7
description: pgvector minor version bump. Used internally for the Beacon similarity search (rule deduplication).

title: Quarterly secrets rotation
description: Standard quarterly rotation: AWS access keys for service accounts, Datadog API keys, Sentry DSN, Postgres replication credentials, internal HMAC keys for service-to-service auth.

comments.author: emp_081
comments.body: All rotated. SOC 2 evidence updated. No service interruption.

title: Investigate Pylon Suggest grounding requirements
description: Investigate which classes of Beacon rules can produce mechanically-correct fix suggestions vs. those that require human judgment. Output: rule taxonomy + criteria for 'suggest-ready'.

comments.author: emp_011
comments.body: Tagging spikes as no-AC by convention; output is a doc, not a feature.

comments.author: emp_011
comments.body: Doc landed (internal: docs/research/pylon-suggest-grounding.md). TL;DR: of our 280 default rules, ~60 are mechanically suggestable (single-line fixes with deterministic transformation). Another ~80 are conditionally suggestable (need codebase context). Remaining ~140 are not suggestable in any meaningful way without dialogue with the developer.

title: Survey Java static-analysis libraries for Q3 2026 prep
description: Survey existing Java static-analysis libraries (SpotBugs, Error Prone, PMD, Checker Framework, etc.) to inform the build-vs-buy decision for Java language coverage. Aligned with OQ-5 in the Master PRD.

comments.author: emp_011
comments.body: Doc landed. Recommendation: hire (don't acquire). The existing libraries are fine for some patterns but none of them have the rule-customization story Pylon needs to differentiate. Marcus + I agreed; OQ-5 resolved as 'hire' — adding to Q3 hiring plan.

title: Beacon rule-conflict detector design
description: Design spike for the rule-conflict resolver feature in the Q1 2027 roadmap. When two rules disagree on the same code (one says 'use X', another says 'avoid X'), Pylon should surface this and help the customer choose. Spike output: design doc.

comments.author: emp_080
comments.body: Initial draft circulated. Detection turns out to be more subtle than expected — 'conflict' isn't binary; rules can be partially-overlapping. Iterating with Hugo.
